content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 2140A • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

June 28, 1976

George Alan Rekers, Ph.D
875 Westlake Boulevard
Suite Number 111
Westlake Village, California 91361

Dear Dr. Rekers:

Thank you for your letter of June, 1976, and the enclosed
copies of two mass mailings for your campaign for the Las Virgenes
Municipal Water District Board of Directors.

The provision of California's Political Reform Act, requiring
copies of mass mailings to be sent to the Fair Political Practices
Commission is Government Code Section 84305 which states, in per-
tinent part:

.....a copy of every mass mailing in support of or in
opposition to a state candidate or state measure shall
be sent to the commission on the same day on which
it is mailed to the public generally. Such copies
sent to the commission shall be public records.

Please note that the only copies of mass mailings which are
required to be sent to the Commission headquarters in Sacramento
are those supporting or opposing a state candidate or state mea-
sure. However, Government Code Section 84305 also provides
additional requirements pertaining to methods of sending mass
mailings and identifying the sender, and these requirements are
applicable to both state and local candidates. The enclosure
entitled "Mass Mailings" provides an explanation of the require-
ments contained in the Political Reform Act.

I hope this letter and the enclosure are helpful. If I can
be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

John C. Greenwood
Program Coordinator

lh

Enclosure

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-2406

sections.question_synthetic: What are the requirements under California's Political Reform Act for submitting copies of mass mailings to the FPPC, particularly for a local water district campaign?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Only mass mailings supporting or opposing state candidates or state measures must be sent to FPPC headquarters in Sacramento. However, all mass mailings (state and local) must comply with additional requirements regarding delivery methods and sender identification.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections found; extracted question and conclusion from letter content. Document is clearly an FPPC advisory response with specific statutory citation and guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the requirements under California's Political Reform Act for submitting copies of mass mailings to the FPPC, particularly for a local water district campaign?

CONCLUSION: Only mass mailings supporting or opposing state candidates or state measures must be sent to FPPC headquarters in Sacramento. However, all mass mailings (state and local) must comply with additional requirements regarding delivery methods and sender identification.
embedding.first_500_words: June 28, 1976 George Alan Rekers, Ph.D 875 Westlake Boulevard Suite Number 111 Westlake Village, California 91361 Dear Dr. Rekers: Thank you for your letter of June, 1976, and the enclosed copies of two mass mailings for your campaign for the Las Vigenes Municipal Water District Board of Directors. The provision of California's Political Reform Act requiring copies of mass mailings to be sent to the Fair Political Practices Commission is Government Code Section 84305 which states, in pertinent part: ...a copy of every mass mailing in support of or in opposition to a state candidate or state measure shall be sent to the commission on the same day on which it is mailed to the public generally. Such copies sent to the commission shall be public records. Please note that the only copies of mass mailings which are required to be sent to the Commission headquarters in Sacramento are those supporting or opposing a state candidate or state measure. However, Government Code Section 84305 also provides additional requirements pertaining to methods of sending mass mailings and identifying the sender, and these requirements are applicable to both state and local candidates. The enclosure entitled "Mass Mailings" provides an explanation of the requirements contained in the Political Reform Act. I hope this letter and the enclosure are helpful. If I can be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, John C. Greenwood Program Coordinator
embedding.summary: FPPC provides guidance to a candidate for Las Vigenes Municipal Water District regarding mass mailing requirements under Government Code Section 84305, clarifying that copies of mass mailings need only be submitted to FPPC for state-level races, though all mass mailings must comply with identification and delivery method requirements.
